name: eng-org
description: 5-role multi-agent engineering org for any project — EM → Tech Leads → Domain Devs → independent Test agents → specialist Reviewer agents, plus a system Architect that produces versioned ADRs before decomposition. /eng-org:init reads your project, detects stack and domains, then sets up tailored agents, slash commands, governance files with 9 binding guardrails, and a portable context layer (AGENTS.md universal entry point, machine-readable eng-org.json manifest, dated DECISIONS.md logs, a multi-project scaffolder, and the /eng-org:graphyfy skill that draws Mermaid linking-graphs of the whole project) so any AI or engineer can pick the project up cold. New in 0.12.0: Mode L autopilot — an autonomous build-until-done loop. /eng-org:autopilot runs the G-9 clarity gate (8-item scorecard incl. feasibility + dependency pre-flight, interview until 8/8, authority grants, architect plan preview → human approval), then a crash-safe external driver re-invokes /eng-org:autopilot-iterate in a fresh context per iteration: one pipeline stage of one REQ, decide-and-log or park on ambiguity (never asks mid-loop), fingerprint circuit breaker against doom loops, per-milestone retro with validated-only learnings, immutable SPEC/acceptance-criteria zone against reward hacking. Stops at production-ready PR approval — merges and deployment stay human-controlled.
